zh.Whittington, Oswestry, Shropshire, Engla: 英國英格蘭史洛普爾廷頓
zh.Wooten, Warwickshire, England: 英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Edward Grey於1472年1月1日出生在英國斯塔福爾恩維爾時，他的父親Sir Humphrey Grey31歲，母親Anna Fielding28歲。他於1486年與Joyce Horde在英國斯塔福爾恩維爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1529年2月14日在英國斯塔福爾金巴享年57歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾金巴。

zh-hans.Whittington, Oswestry, Shropshire, Engla: 英国英格兰史洛普爾廷頓
zh-hans.Wooten, Warwickshire, England: 英国英格兰瓦維其可爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Edward Grey于1472年1月1日出生在英国斯塔福爾恩維爾时，他的父亲Sir Humphrey Grey31岁，母亲Anna Fielding28岁。他于1486年与Joyce Horde在英国斯塔福爾恩維爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1529年2月14日在英国斯塔福爾金巴享年57岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾金巴。

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Edward Grey was born on 1 January 1472, in Enville, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir Humphrey Grey, was 31 and his mother, Anna Fielding, was 28. He married Joyce Horde in 1486, in Enville, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 14 February 1529, in Kinver, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 57, and was buried in Kinver, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Edward Grey est né le 1 janvier 1472, à Enville, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Humphrey Grey, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Anna Fielding, avait 28 ans. Il a épousé Joyce Horde en 1486, à Enville, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 14 février 1529, à Kinver, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Kinver, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Edward Grey am 1. Januar 1472 in Enville, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Humphrey Grey 31 und seine Mutter Anna Fielding 28. Er hat 1486 in Enville, Staffordshire, England Joyce Horde geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Februar 1529 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Kinver, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Kinver, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Grey è nato il il 1 gennaio 1472 nel Enville, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Humphrey Grey, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Anna Fielding, aveva 28 anni. Lui sposato Joyce Horde nel 1486, nel Enville, Staffordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 14 febbraio 1529, nel Kinver, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolto a Kinver, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Greyが1472年1月1日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエンヴィルで生まれた時，父Sir Humphrey Greyは31歳，母Anna Fieldingは28歳であった。1486年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアエンヴィルでJoyce Hordeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1529年2月14日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアキンバーで57歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアキンバーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Edward Grey(이)가 1472년 1월 1일 영국 스태퍼드실르 빌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Humphrey Grey(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Anna Fielding(은)는 28세였다. 그는 1486년 영국 스태퍼드실르 빌에서 Joyce Horde(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1529년 2월 14일 영국 스태퍼드실르 킨버에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 킨버에 묻혔다.

mn.1 January 1472–14 February 1529: 1472 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 1–1529 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 14

pt.1 January 1472–14 February 1529: 1 de janeiro de 1472–14 de fevereiro de 1529
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Edward Grey nasceu em 1 de janeiro de 1472, em Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Humphrey Grey, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Anna Fielding, tinha 28 anos. Ele casou-se com Joyce Horde em 1486, em Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1529, em Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 57 anos, e foi sepultado em Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Edward Grey родился 1 января 1472 г. в местности энвилл, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Humphrey Grey было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Anna Fielding, 28. Он женился на женщине по имени Joyce Horde в 1486 г. в местности энвилл, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 14 февраля 1529 г. в местности кинвир, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности кинвир, стаффордшир, Англия.

es.1 January 1472–14 February 1529: 1 de enero de 1472–14 de febrero de 1529
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Edward Grey nació el 1 de enero de 1472, en Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Humphrey Grey, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Anna Fielding, tenía 28 años. Se casó con Joyce Horde en 1486, en Enville, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 14 de febrero de 1529, en Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 57 años, y fue sepultado en Kinver, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
